Manufacturing and Trading 
of Consumer Products

The Company deals with the manufacturing and trading of 
consumer durables products which includes Fans, Domestic 
Appliances, Kitchen appliances, and Non-Electrical Kitchen 
Aids.

Manufacturing and Trading 
of Lighting Products

The Company deals with the manufacturing and trading 
of lighting products which includes LED products, such as 
lamps, bulbs, battens and ceiling lights in consumer lighting 
and poles, highmast, street lighting, area lighting, etc. in 
professional lighting
Manufacturing and Trading of Consumer Products
Manufacturing and Trading of Lighting Products

A. Consumer Products Segment

The company manufactures and trades consumer products, including Fans, Domestic Appliances, Kitchen 
Appliances, and Non - Electrical Kitchen Aids. 
The Consumer Products Segment caters to retailers, distributors, wholesalers, institutions, e-commerce, 
international markets, and government sector who sell the Company’s products directly to end-users such as 
consumers, corporates, and small businesses

B. Lighting Solutions Segment
The Lighting Solutions Segment includes professional lighting and consumer lighting. Professional lighting includes commercial and industrial customers who require high-quality and energy-efficient indoor and outdoor lighting solutions for their premises. These customers include contractors, project developers, 
corporate offices, Govt institutions, amongst others. The Company also provides IoT-based experience (smart 
lights) to B2B customers such as smart cities, Urban Local Bodies (ULB), and Government projects, amongst others.
Consumer lighting includes Light-emitting diode (LED) lighting products such as bulbs, battens, and ceiling lights, which cater to consumer household demand. The product portfolio also includes energy-efficient and smart connected LEDs, which cater to smart home sentiments of consumers. Further, interior design, architecture, and home décor trends influence the consumer lighting industry

Diversifying into 
e n v i r o n m e n t a l l y 
sustainable products 
enables Bajaj Electricals 
Limited (BEL) to align 
with global sustainability 
trends, meet evolving 
consumer demand, and 
strengthen its ESG profile. 
Sustainable products drive 
innovation, enhance brand 
value, and open new 
market opportunities, both 
domestic and international. 
This opportunity supports 
national green goals and 
ensures resilient, future ready, and inclusive 
growth for BEL.

Energy management 
creates an opportunity for 
Bajaj Electricals to lower its 
carbon footprint, improve

By enhancing energy

renewable energy, the

Growing consumer

around product impact

Bajaj Electricals has established

Water scarcity poses a 
significant risk, potentially 
leading to operational 
disruptions, higher costs, 
and reduced production

negatively impact the

Bajaj Electricals Limited is 
committed to reducing its water 
footprint as a key aspect of 
enhancing operational efficiency, 
reducing cost and promoting 
environmental sustainability. We 
recycle treated effluent water 
from Sewage Treatment Plants 
(STP) and Effluent Treatment 
Plants (ETP) for use in operations 
and green belt development
To strengthen its water 
stewardship efforts, an expert 
agency has been engaged 
to assess and manage water related risks across all facilities. 
Additionally, a rainwater 
harvesting system has been 
installed at the Nashik unit to 
store, utilize, and recharge the 
groundwater table.

Both the Chakan and Nashik 
units are equipped with Zero 
Liquid Discharge (ZLD) systems, 
reinforcing the company’s 
commitment to sustainable 
water management and 
resource conservation.

Bajaj Electricals Limited 
recognises that climate 
change and broader 
macroeconomic factors 
may significantly impact 
the resilience of its supply 
chain. These disruptions 
could manifest as raw 
material shortages, 
delayed deliveries, and 
increased operational 
costs. Furthermore, 
Environmental, Social, and 
Governance (ESG) risks 
within the supply chain, 
particularly those related 
to raw material sourcing 
and quality, pose potential 
reputational risks to the 
organisation. Proactive 
risk management and 
supplier engagement are 
essential to safeguarding 
business continuity and 
stakeholder trust.

To mitigate these risks, the 
Company implements the 
following sustainable supply 
chain practices such as:
 1. Contingency Planning: 
Develops effective plans to 
address potential supply 
chain disruptions.
2. Supplier Diversification: 
Reduces dependence on 
specific sources, enhancing 
supply chain resilience.
 3. Quality Control: 
Implements stringent 
measures to maintain high 
product standards.
4. Regular Supplier Audits: 
Ensures adherence to ethical 
and sustainable practices.
5. Local Supplier Partnerships: 
Enhance regional 
resilience and support 
community development.
6. Training on ESG: Provides 
regular training to value 
chain partners on the 
importance of ESG.
7. Open Communication: 
Maintains communication 
with suppliers to adapt to 
changing business needs.
 8. ESG Supplier Screening: 
Ensures suppliers align 
with ESG criteria for 
responsible sourcing.

Yes, with effect from April 4, 2025, in order to ensure focused Board-level oversight of the Company’s Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) agenda, the Board of Directors has approved the expansion of the scope of the erstwhile Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Committee. The 
CSR Committee will now also oversee ESG-related initiatives and activities. Accordingly, the Committee has been renamed as the ‘CSR & ESG Committee’ to reflect this enhanced mandate. The composition of the CSR & ESG Committee is as follows: 
(i) Mrs. Pooja Bajaj – Chairperson, 
(ii) Mr. Shekhar Bajaj – Member, 
(iii) Mr. Sudarshan Sampathkumar – Member, and 
(iv) Mr. Saurabh Kumar – Member.

Yes, the Company has an Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Policy to ensure that proper procedures are in place to prevent any 
violations of the law and to clearly outline the responsibilities of the Company’s personnel and business partners in preventing 
bribery and corruption. This policy drives the Company’s Anti-Bribery Management System (“ABMS”), with the primary objective of 
mitigating bribery-related risks by implementing robust processes and controls, training and awareness activities that ensure:
a. Compliance with applicable anti-bribery laws;
b. Creating awareness about the Company’s emphasis on ethical business practices and its zero-tolerance approach towards 
conduct that is in breach of this Policy; and
c. Effective Implementation of ABMS by incident reporting, investigation, and compliance.
Please find the Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Policy at: https://www.bajajelectricals.com/pages/investors
Not applicable, as there were no such cases of corruption and conflict of interest

Yes, the Company maintains a strict policy against unethical conduct and ensures compliance with relevant principles, including those pertaining to conflicts of interest. 
To this end, the Company has adopted a comprehensive Code of Conduct (the “Code”), which is applicable to the Directors and Senior Management of the Company. The Code is publicly available on the Company’s website at: https://www.bajajelectricals.com/pages/investors 
The Code mandates adherence to the highest standards of integrity and ethical behavior. It prohibits acceptance of any benefits from business associates that could compromise, or appear to compromise, objective business judgment. Directors and Senior Management are expected to act honestly, fairly, ethically, and with integrity, and to maintain a professional, courteous, and respectful demeanor at all times.
To reinforce accountability:
The Board of Directors and Senior Management are required to annually affirm compliance with the Code.
A declaration to this effect, signed by the Managing Director & Chief Executive Officer, is annexed to the Corporate Governance Report. Directors and Senior Management are explicitly prohibited from engaging in business activities that create or may create conflicts with their responsibilities to the Company.
In line with Regulation 26(5) of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, members of the senior management are also required to disclose annually to the Board all material, financial, and commercial transactions where they have a personal interest and where such transactions could potentially lead to a conflict of interest with the Company. No instances of corruption or conflict of interest were identified during the reporting period.

As part of its Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) obligations under the Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2022, the Company has partnered with CPCB-authorized recyclers to ensure the responsible management of post-consumer plastic waste. The Company follows reverse logistics systems, ensuring that products and their packaging are collected for recycling or safe disposal at the end of their life cycle. Through this 
collaboration, over 1,400 MT of plastic waste has been successfully collected and recycled on behalf of the Company.

The Company is also fully compliant with the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022. As a producer of electrical and electronic equipment, the Company collaborates with CPCB registered recyclers to manage the collection and recycling of e-waste. We are also conducting consumer awareness programs on the importance of E-waste management through various platforms and detailed information regarding end-of-life product returns included in product labeling. In FY2024-25 around 12,000 MT of e-waste has been collected and recycled through authorized partners.

All hazardous waste generated at manufacturing units is disposed of in compliance with regulatory requirements by transferring it to CPCB-authorized recyclers. Notably, the Chakan and Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar units have achieved “Zero Waste to Landfill” certification from M/s Intertek USA, reflecting the Company’s commitment to responsible waste management

The Company works with CPCB-authorized recyclers to manage various types of waste. To promote effective segregation and recycling, separate bins for Plastic, wet, dry, and other waste are placed across Company facilities

Yes, the Company has an equal opportunity employer policy which reflects the Company’s commitment to ensure equality, create an inclusive workplace & work culture and promote diversity in the workplace in which all individuals are treated with respect and dignity. The Company is an equal opportunity employer providing opportunities without any discrimination on the grounds of age, colour, disability, marital status, nationality, region, race, caste, religion, place of birth, sex, sexual orientation, and any sort of family 
history. Individuals are encouraged to report any complaints/violations of the policy by writing to the HR Compliance team. The policy can be accessed at: https://www.bajajelectricals.com/pages/investors

Yes, the Company has dedicated grievance mechanisms in place.	 

For employees the Company has an internal policy and grievance mechanism.	 

For workers, safety committee meetings are conducted at factory level to redress their grievances.	 

For women employees and workers, the Company has an ICC (Internal Complaints Committee) formed under POSH and a common Grievance redressal committee for any other grievances.	 

The Company’s vigil mechanism allows reporting of genuine concerns or grievances regarding any poor or unacceptable practice, as well as incidents of misconduct.

Yes, the Company has implemented a comprehensive Health and Safety Management System and remains firmly committed to fostering a culture of safety and well-being across all its plants, warehouse, offices and all our operational sites.
To uphold the highest safety standards, the Company conducts regular safety rounds using the ‘One EHS’ digital tool, which enables seamless reporting of unsafe acts and conditions. This proactive mechanism ensures swift identification and mitigation of potential hazards, reinforcing a secure and compliant work environment.
The Company also conducts routine safety audits, including specialized fire safety audits, to rigorously evaluate and enhance existing safety protocols. These efforts are supported by regular compliance checks to ensure adherence to all legal and regulatory health and safety requirements.
Further, comprehensive training programs are carried out for employees, covering topics related to occupational safety, environmental stewardship, and health awareness. Firefighting and first-aid training are also provided to empower employees with the skills needed to respond effectively to emergencies.
 
To safeguard employee health, periodic medical check-ups are conducted to enable early detection and prompt care. Through these multi-layered initiatives, the Company strives to achieve its vision of “Zero Accident Sites,” underscoring its unwavering commitment to ensuring a safe, healthy and resilient workplace for all

The Company places a high priority on Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA) for both routine and non-routine activities. The HIRA process is integral to identifying and assessing risks associated with critical equipment. It encompasses several key steps:
Reviewing Incident History: Activities and areas with a history of incidents are carefully examined to identify 
potential safety risks.
Stakeholder Consultation: Engaging relevant stakeholders to gain valuable insights and enhance their understanding of safety concerns.
Material Safety Data Sheets: Analyzing safety data related to plant machinery and equipment to identify potential hazards and required safety precautions
These steps allow the Company to proactively identify hazards and take necessary actions to maintain a safer workplace.
The Company employs a “Probability-Consequence” Risk Matrix to assess the severity of identified risks. Once a hazard is identified, its severity is evaluated in consultation with personnel who may be exposed to the risk. If a hazard is categorized as high-risk, immediate action is taken, beginning with short-term control measures, followed by a detailed assessment to determine long-term solutions. The outcomes of these assessments guide the implementation of effective risk control measures, which are prioritized as follows:
Elimination of Hazard: Completely removing the source of danger from the workplace to eliminate risk at its root.
Substitution of Hazard: Replacing hazardous substances, processes, or materials with safer alternatives to reduce potential harm.
Isolation or Enclosure of Hazard: Containing or segregating the hazard, for example, by restricting access to high-risk areas such as chemical storage rooms.
Engineering Solutions: Redesigning equipment, workspaces, or processes to minimize exposure to hazards through technical controls.
Administrative Procedures: Establishing safe work practices, such as job rotation, shift planning, and task diversification to limit the duration and frequency of exposure.
Use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and Training: Supplying suitable PPE and ensuring comprehensive 
training on its correct use when other control measures cannot fully eliminate risk.
Through these systematic measures, the Company ensures the identification, mitigation and continuous management of workplace hazards, fostering a safer and healthier environment for all employees

Bajaj Electricals Limited is steadfast in its commitment to fostering a culture of safety and well-being across its operations. The Company upholds the highest standards of workplace safety through regular safety rounds and the use of its 'One EHS' tool, enabling seamless reporting of unsafe acts and conditions. This proactive system allows for the swift identification and resolution of potential hazards, reinforcing a secure and compliant work environment.
Comprehensive safety audits—including specialized fire audits—are routinely conducted to evaluate and strengthen safety protocols. These are complemented by regular compliance checks and extensive training programs focused on safety, environmental, and health topics. The Company also ensures emergency preparedness through dedicated fire-fighting and first-aid training. With the ultimate goal of achieving “Zero Accident Sites,” these multifaceted initiatives reflect Bajaj Electricals’ unwavering dedication to creating a safe and resilient workplace.
In parallel, the Company has implemented a robust health and wellness strategy. Regular medical checkups are conducted in accordance with statutory requirements, supported by an experienced, registered medical practitioner stationed at its factories. 
Recognizing the importance of mental well-being, Bajaj Electricals also organizes stress management sessions at its headquarters, equipping employees with tools to navigate workplace challenges effectively.
To foster a culture of health consciousness, the Company conducts informative wellness sessions and health awareness programs that encourage healthy lifestyle choices. Moreover, physical and mental well-being are promoted through team-building and sports activities, which not only boost employee morale but also strengthen camaraderie and mental resilience within the workforce.
Through these comprehensive and holistic initiatives, Bajaj Electricals Limited continues to champion the safety, health, and overall well-being of its people

The contract between the Company and its value chain partners incorporates statutory provisions, including payment and deduction of statutory dues such as Goods and Services Tax, Tax Deducted at Source, etc. The value chain partners are primarily responsible for adherence to various statutes. The central team, along with the branches, monitors such deductions on a regular basis to ensure that the same are deposited with the government authorities. Depositing of statutory dues and filing of returns by value chain partners are cross-checked by the system with data available on Government portals. In case any deficiency/ mismatch 
is observed, the respective team takes up with the value chain partners for review and corrections. In a few cases, payment of GST and TCS is made to vendors only after respective payments are reflected at the relevant government portals.
The Company also conducts regular structured audits and reviews to ensure compliance with statutory obligations pertaining to workers in our value chain, including the timely payment of wages and social security benefits. If any non-compliance occurs, strict measures are taken against business partners who fail to meet these obligations

Risks and concerns identified during supplier evaluations are discussed with the respective suppliers. They are required to implement corrective actions, which are then reviewed by our team for re-evaluation and closure of action points.

The Company takes a people-centric and stakeholder-inclusive approach to value creation. At Bajaj Electricals, stakeholder engagement is an important aspect of the Company’s overall decision-making process. The Company identifies its key stakeholders by prioritizing the list of stakeholder groups based on their ability to influence and to get influenced by the Company’s performance and operations. Identified stakeholders include board and leadership, investors, customers, vendors, employees, and communities. 
The Company engages through tailored engagement strategies with each stakeholder groups. These stakeholder engagement and feedback mechanism help the Company in identifying and addressing stakeholder concerns

Our organisation engages with stakeholders through various internal teams that actively interact with them to understand their concerns and gather feedback on economic, environmental, social, and other relevant matters. This input is systematically channelled to the Board through regular business review meetings and the enterprise risk management framework, ensuring the Board remains informed of key stakeholder perspectives and emerging issues. Further, the Stakeholder Relationship Committee, the CSR & ESG Committee, and the Risk Management Committee of the Company play a pivotal role in fostering continuous, structured engagement with stakeholders and serve as conduits for relaying critical feedback and developments to the Board on an ongoing basis.

Yes, at Bajaj Electricals, we actively incorporate stakeholder inputs in the identification and management of environmental and social topics, recognizing them as essential partners in our mission to create long-term value. We have established a well-defined stakeholder engagement and consultation process, outlining key engagement platforms and the frequency of interaction with both internal and external stakeholders. Their insights play a pivotal role in shaping our policies and guiding our initiatives.
Our commitment to meaningful engagement drives consistent and transparent communication across various channels, enabling us to understand evolving stakeholder expectations. We regularly seek feedback on our performance and offerings, helping to enhance our operational effectiveness and align our approach with stakeholder priorities.
Our business and ESG strategies are thoughtfully tailored to address the critical concerns of our stakeholders, ensuring mutual benefit and sustainable growth.
Further details are available in the Stakeholder Engagement section of our Annual Report.

The COC Committee reviews each case and recommends actions to the Managing Director & Chief Executive Officer (MD & CEO). Additionally, any grievance concerning a member of the COC Committee should be directed to the MD & CEO.

Yes, Bajaj Electricals Limited is undertaking concerted and strategic efforts to reduce its greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions, as part of its broader commitment to environmental sustainability. The Company is steadily transitioning towards cleaner and more sustainable energy sources, with a particular focus on renewable energy integration.
To reduce its environmental footprint and enhance energy efficiency, solar panels have been installed across all manufacturing units. 
This initiative not only supports energy self-reliance but also significantly contributes to lowering carbon emissions. In line with this, the Company has conducted detailed energy audits at its Chakan and Nashik facilities through Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE)-accredited energy auditors. These audits help identify further opportunities for energy conservation and efficiency improvements. 
Several energy-efficient technologies and process optimizations have been implemented across operations, including:
Transition from LPG to PNG in the new paint shop at the Chakan unit, enabling cleaner combustion and improved operational efficiency.
Installation of Variable Frequency Drives (VFDs) and energy-efficient motors, reducing energy consumption by optimizing motor speed and torque.
Use of aluminum piping for the compressed air system at the Chakan facility, which minimizes pressure drops and improves air flow efficiency.
Waste heat recovery systems in paint booths to capture and reuse heat energy, significantly reducing fuel usage.Deployment of energy-efficient air compressors, which further contribute to reduced electricity consumption and operational costs.
Employees awareness through continuous training and reward & recognition mechanism which helps us to drive the decarbonisation initiative across the organisation
Through these focused interventions, Bajaj Electricals continues to embed sustainability into its operations, actively reducing its carbon footprint and contributing to the global transition towards a low-carbon economy.

Bajaj Electricals Limited is deeply committed to implementing a responsible, forward-thinking, and sustainable waste management strategy that aligns with industry best practices. The Company’s approach emphasizes the systematic segregation of waste, safe handling, and eco-friendly disposal methods to minimize environmental impact across all operations.
Adoption of the Waste Management Hierarchy
The Company follows the globally recognized Hierarchy of Waste Management Practices, prioritizing actions that have the least environmental impact:
Avoidance/Reduction: Preventing waste generation at the source through improved operational efficiency and 
sustainable practices.
Reuse: Extending the life cycle of materials by using them again for the same or different purposes without altering their form.
Recycling: Converting waste materials into new products or raw materials, thereby conserving natural resources.
Recovery: Extracting valuable energy or materials from waste that cannot be reused or recycled.
Disposal: As a last resort, disposing of residual waste in an environmentally compliant manner
Waste Segregation and Handling
Waste is segregated at the source in all facilities using color-coded and clearly labeled containers. Employees trained in safe waste handling practices manage the transfer of waste to designated scrap yards while adhering to strict safety protocols and using appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE). All segregated waste is then routed to certified vendors for appropriate recycling, reuse, or scientific disposal in line with sustainable practices.
Hazardous Waste Management
The hazardous waste generated, including shot blasting dust, paint sludge, ETP sludge, used oil, empty drums and barrels, contaminated cotton rags, and other such materials, is managed with the utmost responsibility. Disposal is carried out in compliance with the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) and respective State Pollution Control Board (SPCB) regulations. Bajaj Electricals is a certified member of the Common Hazardous Waste Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facility (CHWTSDF) at MEPL, Ranjangaon, 
ensuring safe, scientific, and regulatory-compliant disposal.
Non-Hazardous Waste and Thermocol Reduction Initiative
In alignment with its Refuse-Reduce–Reuse–Recycle-Rotten (5R) principles, the Company has taken significant steps to eliminate non-biodegradable materials from its supply chain. A standout initiative includes the reduction of thermocol usage for packaging by replacing it with environmentally friendly pulp-based alternatives. This initiative, driven through the Company’s non-profit arm, 
“Bajaj Paryavaran Mitra”, founded in 2004, fosters environmental stewardship. All pulp packaging waste is returned, adhering to circular economy principles.
Waste Management and EPR Compliance
As a responsible producer, the Company upholds its Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) under the Plastic, Battery & E-Waste Management Rules. Bajaj Electricals is registered with CPCB for the collection, recycling, and environmentally sound disposal of electrical and electronic equipment. The Company also complies strictly with the Reduction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) directives, ensuring product safety and minimal environmental impact. Waste management protocols at the Nashik and Chakan 
facilities ensure that all e-waste is disposed of through authorized agencies, maintaining full regulatory compliance

Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) System
Reinforcing its commitment to sustainable water use, the Company has implemented a state-of-the-art Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) system. This includes advanced Effluent Treatment Plants (ETP), Reverse Osmosis (RO) systems, and Evaporation Units that work in tandem to treat and recycle process wastewater. This integrated system helps conserve water resources and ensures compliance 
with the most stringent environmental standards.
Sewage Treatment and Water Reuse
Modern Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) at the Chakan and Nashik facilities treat domestic wastewater, abiding by high quality standards. The treated water is repurposed for green belt development within plant premises, contributing to a circular water usage system and reducing freshwater demand.
Innovation and Continuous Improvement
Bajaj Electricals fosters a culture of continuous innovation in its waste management efforts. The R&D team actively collaborates with suppliers and vendors to ensure that our products meet RoHS compliance standards. At the Chakan facility, new technologies 
have been introduced in the paint shop to reuse recovery powder, minimizing waste generation. Furthermore, a secure, dedicated hazardous waste storage room has been established to ensure safe containment and management of hazardous materials
As the Company does not operate in or around ecologically sensitive areas, there are no environmental approvals or clearances to report.

Bajaj Electricals has a comprehensive Business Continuity and Disaster Management Plan covering critical business functions across manufacturing plants, warehouses, offices, and IT infrastructure. All our applications have been migrated to the cloud, with approximately 90% hosted on Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platforms. Notably, we have adopted SAP RISE (cloud) for our core ERP operations, which offers enhanced resilience, scalability, and integrated disaster recovery features. This cloud-first strategy significantly strengthens our business continuity posture. Cloud and SaaS platforms offer built-in 
redundancies, automated backups, rapid failover, and geographically distributed infrastructure, resulting in minimal downtime and faster recovery during disruptions. Key applications have defined recovery mechanisms, and our IT team conducts regular Disaster Recovery (DR) drills to validate readiness and response capabilities.The Risk Management Committee periodically reviews and updates the plan to reflect emerging threats. Additionally, structured stakeholder communication protocols ensure that roles, responsibilities, and critical updates are shared in a timely manner during disaster scenarios. This integrated approach minimises operational impact, ensures continuity of services, and reinforces Bajaj 
Electricals’ commitment to proactive risk management and resilience.”

The Company follows a robust mechanism to track consumer complaints and the focus has been to make it easy for consumer voices to be heard and accordingly, a well-established escalation matrix has been developed on the Company’s website. In case of a consumer complaint, each escalation is addressed promptly and followed up for resolution. Any negative response coming during the consumer survey is also addressed by calling the consumer and understanding where the Company failed to meet expectations. The Company takes measures to address consumer feedback
Consumer Meets:  To gauge consumers’ satisfaction level regarding products and services.
Consumer satisfaction feedback: To analyse consumer feedback and take suitable measures for upgrading products and services. A system of collecting feedback through feedback cards, e-mails, 
telephonic surveys, etc.
Consumer Care Cell: 24x7 toll-free number for receiving and redressing consumer complaints.
‘Bajaj Paddy’- Virtual assistant on the website of the Company: To stimulate conversations with consumers and to help them report a problem with an appliance, request a demo or schedule an appointment with a technician for installation and generate consumer complaint instantly in real time.
Product check-up and consumer meets: To address consumers’ concerns and complaints.

For consumer products, the Company receives request calls for tech support and other queries including product installation, product demo, repairs/warranty claims, etc. All such requests are attended to with utmost seriousness and the entire organisation foucses on reducing the hassle to consumers. Out of close to 25 lakh consumer request calls received during the year, ~more than 96% were resolved at the end of the year. The Company resolves such requests of consumers in the following manner:

A. Tech Support
Bajaj Electricals has adopted various support methods for consumers to reach out to the Company such as Voice Call through Call centres, Web Form and Online Chat Bots at Bajaj Electricals and Morphy Richards India website and e-mail support. The Company has deployed the web-based CRM for capturing consumer calls online. The consumer calls are registered online on a real time basis and almost 95%+ calls are resolved within two days of call registration. A confirmatory message is sent to each 
consumer with call registration ID instantly on registering the call.

B. Consumer Satisfaction and Feedback
The Company reaches out to consumers to gauge and track consumer satisfaction level. Consumer satisfaction is measured through a digital process. The Company has managed to maintain a delighted and satisfied response rate above 99% in all these months. Further, the Company constantly monitors social media, and any online client concerns and takes immediate action to address the concerns.

The Company takes multiple proactive steps to inform and educate consumers about the safe and responsible usage of its electrical 
appliances and services.
All products are accompanied by clear product labelling and comprehensive user manuals, which contain detailed instructions on 
safe installation, operation, and maintenance. These manuals are available both in physical form with the product and digitally on 
the Company’s website, ensuring easy access for all consumers.
To further reinforce safe usage, customer service representatives and technicians undergo periodic training focused on consumer education, safety protocols, and product-specific handling guidelines. This enables them to provide accurate guidance to consumers during installation visits or service interactions.
The Company also makes use of digital platforms and automated tools such as the virtual assistant ‘Bajaj Paddy’ on the website to assist consumers with troubleshooting, demo scheduling, and service requests, while ensuring safety-related information is conveyed in an interactive and user-friendly manner.
In addition, product demo sessions, service calls, and consumer meets often include guidance on safe usage, energy efficiency tips, and do’s and don’ts related to electrical appliances. These interactions provide a platform to resolve queries and reinforce responsible use in a real-world context.
For specific appliances with higher voltage requirements or safety sensitivities, consumers are also guided on grounding, circuit load management, and warranty conditions that depend on proper usage.
The Company also uses SMS and email communications to provide reminders for maintenance, servicing, and safety checks where applicable, thereby encouraging preventive care and reducing risks associated with improper usage.Through a combination of physical documentation, digital support, trained personnel, and direct consumer outreach, the Company ensures that customers are empowered to use its products safely and responsibly.

Wherever applicable and available, the Company provides additional product information beyond statutory requirements to enhance customer awareness and satisfaction. This includes usage guidelines, care and maintenance instructions, energy efficiency ratings, and details of value-added features that may benefit the consumer
